mnemonics: Powers Produce Positive Probabilities: A regular matrix has some power (n) where every entry is Positive (>0), guaranteeing it will lead to a steady state.
keyPoints: A stochastic matrix is regular if some power of it (P^n) has only positive entries (all > 0).
Every regular stochastic matrix has a unique steady-state probability vector.
The system's state vector converges to this steady-state vector, regardless of the initial starting state.
commonMistakes: Assuming all stochastic matrices are regular (some have absorbing states or periodic cycles).
Confusing 'regular' with 'irreducible'; a regular matrix is always irreducible, but an irreducible matrix is not necessarily regular.
